					<section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-49e2c10e elementor-section-height-min-height elementor-section-stretched elementor-section-boxed elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-items-middle" data-id="49e2c10e" data-element_type="section" data-e-type="section" data-settings="{"background_background":"classic","stretch_section":"section-stretched"}" style="width: 1280px; left: 0px;">
						<div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
					<div class="elementor-column elementor-col-100 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-68e455a8" data-id="68e455a8" data-element_type="column" data-e-type="column">
			<div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-29b857b elementor-widget elementor-widget-theme-post-title elementor-page-title elementor-widget-heading" data-id="29b857b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="theme-post-title.default">
					<h1 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Robustness Tests and Checks for Algorithmic Trading Strategies | Complete Guide</h1>				</div>
					</div>
		</div>
					</div>
		</section>
				<section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-75d3103e blog-content-wrap elementor-section-boxed elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-height-default" data-id="75d3103e" data-element_type="section" data-e-type="section">
						<div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
					<div class="elementor-column elementor-col-50 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-5eb35ef6 blog-content" data-id="5eb35ef6" data-element_type="column" data-e-type="column">
			<div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4ebf1229 elementor-widget elementor-widget-theme-post-content" data-id="4ebf1229" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="theme-post-content.default">
							<div data-elementor-type="wp-post" data-elementor-id="17738" class="elementor elementor-17738" data-elementor-post-type="post">
						<section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-2be7c29a elementor-section-boxed elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-height-default" data-id="2be7c29a" data-element_type="section" data-e-type="section">
						<div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
					<div class="elementor-column elementor-col-100 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-2dd4e994" data-id="2dd4e994" data-element_type="column" data-e-type="column">
			<div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2a9303ce elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="2a9303ce" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">What is a Robust Trading Strategy?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-72e66d81 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="72e66d81" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									A robust trading strategy can withstand the tests of time and evolving market conditions. A large key to successful algorithmic trading lies in gauging how likely a strategy is to fail in new market conditions. A trading strategy that can deal with market shocks, volatility, changes, and price swings is a robust strategy.  <strong>Robustness can be equated to reliability</strong>. Robustness Testing is key to identifying potential reliable trading systems.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2cd1d63d elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="2cd1d63d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">What is a Robustness Test?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-575fe01f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="575fe01f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									A stress test or statistical test to help validate or break an algorithmic trading strategy prior to risking real capital. Robustness tests help identify trading strategies that may fail in live trading prior to risking hard earned money. Avoiding bad trades and trading systems can be equally as important to reaching your trading goals as finding good strategies.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6584740f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6584740f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Trading losses make trading so much harder! <a href="https://www.forbes.com/profile/warren-buffett/?sh=5f432ed64639" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Warren Buffet</a>, the greatest investor to ever live, is quoted as saying, “<em>the first rule of an investment is never lose money. And the second rule of an investment is never forget the first rule.</em>”								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1cd9ee53 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1cd9ee53" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Robustness testing or checks are stress test methods for measuring reliability in systems. These methods have the ability to identify lying <a href="https://buildalpha.com/backtesting-trading-strategies/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">backtests</a>, determine sufficient risk capital, and avoid catastrophic strategies before you potentially lose real capital.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7973ad74 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="7973ad74" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Why are Robustness Checks important in Algorithmic Trading?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6b0f88a8 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6b0f88a8" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Too many algorithmic traders believe a backtest is enough to take a strategy live. Failing to check your strategy’s robustness can lead to substantial and quick losses when moving your strategy from the development stage to live trading.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7b59eb4e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="7b59eb4e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									A robustness check can help reduce the probabilities that a trader risks his hard-earned money on a lying backtest aka a strategy doomed to fail. <strong>Identifying strategies doomed to fail can help traders save capital.</strong>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1df0d047 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1df0d047" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<em>Robustness checks allow the trader to make smarter decisions with his capital without being misled by lying backtests.</em>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1edf375c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1edf375c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Additionally, the math behind avoiding losses is convincing								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-11e4c97a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="11e4c97a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If you lose x% what gain is required to break even?								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5a149d54 elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="5a149d54" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">10% loss needs to gain 11.11%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">20% loss needs to gain 25.00%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">30% loss needs to gain 42.86%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">40% loss needs to gain 66.67%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">50% loss needs to gain 100.00%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">75% loss needs to gain 300.00%</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">90% loss needs to gain 900.00%</span>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1047667a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1047667a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Warren Buffet is right. Losing money makes trading, and investing, much harder. If we have methods to help avoid losses, then what are they?								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-25fba113 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="25fba113" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Do Robustness Tests and Checks guarantee Trading Success?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5506c4de elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5506c4de" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Nothing can guarantee trading or investing success. In this guide, I will walk through various robustness methods and checks that can help raise the probabilities we only risk capital on strategies with high chances of success. Nothing is a guarantee but shifting the odds in our favor is often good enough.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4a9608da elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="4a9608da" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">What are the best Robustness Tests for Algorithmic Trading Systems?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1a389d3d elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="1a389d3d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Out of Sample Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Randomized Out of Sample Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Vs. Random</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Vs. Others</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Vs. Shifted</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Noise Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Monte Carlo Analysis</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Monte Carlo Reshuffle</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Monte Carlo Permutation</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Variance Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Delayed Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Liquidity Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Walk Forward Analysis</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Parameter Optimization / Parameter Stability Testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Noise Testing Parameter Optimization</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Stress Testing with Synthetic Data</span>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-253072cd elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="253072cd" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Each of these robustness techniques will be covered in detail in this guide. Feel free to skip ahead but I would not recommend it! First let’s cover some basics.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-75257d34 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="75257d34" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Why do my trading algorithm strategies fail?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1820caa7 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1820caa7" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Over the last ten years of professional trading experience and the last five plus years of interacting with the Build Alpha community, I have found there are two main reasons that trading strategies fail         								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1b1a2dc6 elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="1b1a2dc6" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">1. Built for one market environment</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">2. Overfit to the noise (overoptimized)</span>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-21ebd5ae elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="21ebd5ae" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									There are certainly other reasons, but let’s take a moment to break down these two as they are undoubtedly the most common.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4a902d43 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4a902d43" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									First, most strategy developers fail to include context into their strategy. Is it a bull market? Are interest rates rising or falling? Is inflation increasing or decreasing? Is the economy doing well? The market and big money players need large signals to move large amounts of money.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7cedfe3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="7cedfe3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Most individual algo traders design strategies around price action signals or technical indicators and <strong>fail to account for market context</strong>. When the market changes, these strategies inevitably suffer.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-15f10d9b elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="15f10d9b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img fetchpriority="high" decoding="async" width="300" height="208" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/fe1b8371-d907-44d7-82cd-0883e5c08006-300x208.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17740 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/fe1b8371-d907-44d7-82cd-0883e5c08006-300x208.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/fe1b8371-d907-44d7-82cd-0883e5c08006-600x417.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/fe1b8371-d907-44d7-82cd-0883e5c08006.avif 625w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f9f9f8" style="--dominant-color: #f9f9f8">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-34a63360 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="34a63360" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Second, many strategy developers become obsessed with getting the best backtesting results possible. Traders will tweak strategy parameters until the backtest’s equity curve moves from the lower left to the upper right with zero turbulence. Ah, the perfect strategy!

								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-178c802b elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="178c802b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img decoding="async" width="300" height="212" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/d86d41aa-da07-45bc-b145-f3304d58cc38-300x212.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17741 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/d86d41aa-da07-45bc-b145-f3304d58cc38-300x212.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/d86d41aa-da07-45bc-b145-f3304d58cc38.avif 558w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="fafbfa" style="--dominant-color: #fafbfa">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-55864db1 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="55864db1" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									These unrealistic expectations cause traders to overfit the history data. Is there a reason the 37-period moving average should work best? What if the 36-period and 38-period result in bad results and negative net profit or profit factor?								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3c0468f7 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="3c0468f7" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The live data will <strong>ALWAYS</strong> be different than the history data, so any strategy that needs new data to be nearly identical to the historical will fail in real trading and cause significant risk.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6ea40d2c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6ea40d2c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If you are looking to build your first automated trading strategy, add context to an existing strategy or want to see a complete guide to building automated trading systems, please check out this: <a href="https://buildalpha.com/algorithmic-trading-guide" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><u>Algorithmic Trading Complete Guide</u></a>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-781168cb elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="781168cb" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">What is Overfitting and Over-optimization in strategy development?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-9e36c4e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="9e36c4e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Overfitting is creating a strategy that too perfectly fits the historical market data and will not generalize well on new unseen data. A trading strategy that memorizes the noise patterns of the historical data will fail when applied to new or unseen data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-153f03de elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="153f03de" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									An overfit strategy just learns the exact cadence of the historical market data but nothing useable going forward.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-70b17a61 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="70b17a61" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img decoding="async" width="300" height="162" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f1041350-0181-4de6-9699-f6d87a5a107e-300x162.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17742 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f1041350-0181-4de6-9699-f6d87a5a107e-300x162.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f1041350-0181-4de6-9699-f6d87a5a107e-600x323.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f1041350-0181-4de6-9699-f6d87a5a107e.avif 702w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="d5d6d8" style="--dominant-color: #d5d6d8">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-299c3a61 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="299c3a61" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									It is very unlikely the data actually created this cat. It is even more unlikely the next set of plots will create a milk saucer. Bad trading advice.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-337154df elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="337154df" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="204" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/26107e1e-a538-4cb5-8a7d-f5b01d1e9f8a-768x245.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17743 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/26107e1e-a538-4cb5-8a7d-f5b01d1e9f8a-768x245.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/26107e1e-a538-4cb5-8a7d-f5b01d1e9f8a-300x96.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/26107e1e-a538-4cb5-8a7d-f5b01d1e9f8a-600x192.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/26107e1e-a538-4cb5-8a7d-f5b01d1e9f8a.avif 895w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f3f2f4" style="--dominant-color: #f3f2f4">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6343863a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6343863a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Many strategies have parameters that can be tweaked to improve the strategy’s historical performance. For example, a simple moving average system may buy when price moves above the 50-period moving average and sell when price falls below the 50-period moving average.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2f45b8c6 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2f45b8c6" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Traders will optimize this parameter setting of 50 to create the best possible backtest. Perhaps the best backtest results are generated when buying on a cross of the 63-period moving average and selling when price falls below the 12-period moving average.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5618787b elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="5618787b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="274" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/e736f72e-804e-4e7f-8686-07d1f5863bf6-768x329.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17744 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/e736f72e-804e-4e7f-8686-07d1f5863bf6-768x329.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/e736f72e-804e-4e7f-8686-07d1f5863bf6-300x129.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/e736f72e-804e-4e7f-8686-07d1f5863bf6-600x257.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/e736f72e-804e-4e7f-8686-07d1f5863bf6.avif 975w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="dde6ef" style="--dominant-color: #dde6ef">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-51146b73 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="51146b73" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Another example of overoptimizing would be to have very specific values such as a stop loss of $217.34 or using a signal such as 12-period RSI crosses above 66.19. These values may produce excellent historical winning trades but are surely meaningless as we go live, and we have unknown data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-10f8e77b elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="10f8e77b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									This is particularly common in forex trading and strategy vendors.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-149d0cb4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="149d0cb4" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="264" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a2726012-d3e4-4c1d-a9ee-b0bde3be0d8b-768x317.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17745 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a2726012-d3e4-4c1d-a9ee-b0bde3be0d8b-768x317.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a2726012-d3e4-4c1d-a9ee-b0bde3be0d8b-300x124.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a2726012-d3e4-4c1d-a9ee-b0bde3be0d8b-600x247.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a2726012-d3e4-4c1d-a9ee-b0bde3be0d8b.avif 975w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="dde6ef" style="--dominant-color: #dde6ef">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-27ba9a80 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="27ba9a80" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Do not optimize every penny or hundredth to find the “holy grail”. It is the surest way to overfitting.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5a919d63 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5a919d63" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									But how can we know if we have over optimized or found ideal strategy parameters? That is the need for Robustness testing or strategy robustness!								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4bdc744 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="4bdc744" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">What are the best robustness tests for algo trading strategies?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5e92e2fc elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5e92e2fc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									There is no single best test and depending on the strategy’s characteristics other statistical tests may be more valuable to what you are trying to disprove than others. For example, a trend following strategy should be tested a different way than a mean reversion strategy. Let’s walk through some examples and circle back to identify a good strategy.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-71017fa5 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="71017fa5" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">In Sample and Out of Sample Testing</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-18d0bc44 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="18d0bc44" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The simplest test we can implement as a first line of defense against curve fitting is an in-sample and out-of-sample split. Most traders use the first 70% of their historical price data as the in-sample data and the last 30% as their out-of-sample data.

								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-48d2507d elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="48d2507d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="210" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/9a7bb431-e5bd-4fa0-8306-bed956a30aab-300x210.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17746 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/9a7bb431-e5bd-4fa0-8306-bed956a30aab-300x210.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/9a7bb431-e5bd-4fa0-8306-bed956a30aab-600x420.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/9a7bb431-e5bd-4fa0-8306-bed956a30aab.avif 655w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e8e8f0" style="--dominant-color: #e8e8f0">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4dac520 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4dac520" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									First, build a trading algorithm on the first 70% where you can tweak, optimize input parameters, add or delete rules, etc. Once pleased with the trading algo’s results, we can test on the unseen, untouched out-of-sample data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-406037a6 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="406037a6" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If the strategy performs well on the out-of-sample data then it is a good sign that the strategy may hold up well on live, unseen data.

								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-26eb978a elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="26eb978a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="237" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f-768x284.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17747 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f-768x284.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f-300x111.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f-1024x379.avif 1024w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f-600x222.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/bf9ce310-5b82-4a15-97bb-40dc19c9c91f.avif 1083w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f8f9f8" style="--dominant-color: #f8f9f8">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-38ff5284 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="38ff5284" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									There are many considerations with out of sample testing such as where to split the data, how much data is needed and more. To dive deeper, check out								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-699c14eb elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="699c14eb" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<a href="https://buildalpha.com/out-of-sample-data/">

												<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Out of Sample Data Selection</span>
											</a>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<a href="https://buildalpha.com/out-of-sample-testing/">

												<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Out of Sample Testing</span>
											</a>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ojXrayois5A&t=162s" target="_blank">

												<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Out of Sample and Overfitting – Build Alpha</span>
											</a>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5d4ec181 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="5d4ec181" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Randomized Out of Sample Testing</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-17cce5ba elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="17cce5ba" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									What if the out-of-sample data was the beginning of a monster bull market? Is it possible our great out-of-sample results are lucky and we are at high risk? Any strategy could have done well during this out-of-sample period.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-de06c05 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="de06c05" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									This is certainly a risk with out-of-sample testing, but we know how to combat this! If you do not wisely select your in and out of sample split, then you could utilize the Randomized Out of Sample test.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-e2969b3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="e2969b3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="221" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/06033eac-9e5e-4bb3-b5ac-d93139cdd8de-300x221.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17748 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/06033eac-9e5e-4bb3-b5ac-d93139cdd8de-300x221.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/06033eac-9e5e-4bb3-b5ac-d93139cdd8de.avif 731w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/06033eac-9e5e-4bb3-b5ac-d93139cdd8de-600x442.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="ebe9f0" style="--dominant-color: #ebe9f0">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-625e6a14 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="625e6a14" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="218" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f25dc03b-abef-4bdb-8fca-49e98e883a0a-300x218.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17749 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f25dc03b-abef-4bdb-8fca-49e98e883a0a-300x218.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f25dc03b-abef-4bdb-8fca-49e98e883a0a-600x436.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/f25dc03b-abef-4bdb-8fca-49e98e883a0a.avif 725w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="eceaf1" style="--dominant-color: #eceaf1">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-485bee09 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="485bee09" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									This test simply selects random bars and assigns them to either the in-sample or out-of-sample portion. Then we trade on our data and take any trades entered on in-sample dates to be the in-sample trades and any trades entered on randomly selected out-of-sample dates to be the out-of-sample trades.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-73c4b72c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="73c4b72c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									To avoid any “luck” during this test, we can repeat 1,000+ times. Here is practical example <a href="https://buildalpha.com/randomized-out-of-sample/">Randomized Out of Sample Testing</a>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7c6f0489 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="7c6f0489" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Vs Random: using the best random trading strategy as a benchmark</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1f30b391 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1f30b391" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									One of my favorite tests, I even mentioned it in the Algorithmic Trading Guide linked above. This test comes from Jaffray Woodriff of Quantitative Investment Management and aims to answer the question, could we have done this well by luck?								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-155f672c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="155f672c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The test generates the best possible random strategy by using random data with real signals, random signals with real data, a combination or mix of the two, etc. The goal for any trading strategy is to have better performance metrics than the best random strategy.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4733f59e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4733f59e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>If our research can beat the best random strategy, then we can have confidence the strategy contains real edge and could not be produced by chance (random).</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6fab4c95 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6fab4c95" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="283" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsRandom-300x283.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-9645 not-transparent" alt="Build Alpha Vs Random test" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsRandom-300x283.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsRandom.avif 598w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e8d5d9" style="--dominant-color: #e8d5d9">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-8d4784f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="8d4784f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Read more about the <a href="https://buildalpha.com/vs-random-test">Vs Random Test</a> here.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-ea97c7a elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="ea97c7a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Vs Other: testing trading strategies on other markets</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7a1da81f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="7a1da81f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Ideally, we want to see trading systems do well across various related markets. A trading strategy on SPY should do well on other markets such as QQQ or DIA. If a strategy performs terribly on related markets, then it is likely the strategy was overfit to the noise in SPY’s historical market data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-625f0a5 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="625f0a5" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="284" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsOthers-300x284.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-9644 not-transparent" alt="Build Alpha Vs Others Test" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsOthers-300x284.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/VsOthers.avif 598w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e3e6e9" style="--dominant-color: #e3e6e9">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6624d3ff elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6624d3ff" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									It is often unnecessary for a strategy to perform well on unrelated markets as market participants may vary drastically creating different data characteristics. Equities have a natural upward drift as companies exist to make money. Oil and corn have natural producers and consumers whereas stocks do not. Forex trading has no central exchange.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-37f59a6a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="37f59a6a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									These differing dynamics bring different traders, hedgers, and investors to the respective markets. These different players leave different footprints thus possibly rendering a strategy that works in one area useless in another.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6583bb80 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6583bb80" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Of course, a strategy that does well across all markets – related or not – is ideal. However, these are often very hard to come by.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1cf780c0 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="1cf780c0" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Vs Shifted: shifting the data to re-test trading strategies</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-338066d elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="338066d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Every charting and data provider uses hourly data from the top of the hour to the end of the hour. That is, 10:00 to 11:00, 11:00 to 12:00, and so on. However, what if we shift the opening and closing time by 1 minute? By 2 minutes, by 3 minutes, etc.? Maybe our shifted data now opens at 10:03 and closes at 11:03, for example.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-661d491e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="661d491e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Now we have additional data that has the same properties but isn’t identical to the historical market data we built our strategy upon. This data is good to evaluate if our strategy requires the exact historical data patterns for success or not.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6a58c0c3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6a58c0c3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Shifting the data works on any timeframe and may change bar patterns, high and lows, consecutive up or down streaks, indicator calculations, and more. A long strategy needing four consecutive red bars and a close in the bottom 20% would trigger a big win on the left but would not enter on the right.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1288d8ea elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1288d8ea" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Re-trading your strategy on shifted data can give insights on how overfit your strategy was to the exact patterns available in the historical market data. Past performance should maintain across shifted data sets. If performance degrades on shifted data, then it may be a warning sign your strategy is over optimized.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6b3fb7f8 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6b3fb7f8" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="298" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a33c3969-a2ec-4710-acfb-367b46b3b10a-768x358.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17750 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a33c3969-a2ec-4710-acfb-367b46b3b10a-768x358.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a33c3969-a2ec-4710-acfb-367b46b3b10a-300x140.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a33c3969-a2ec-4710-acfb-367b46b3b10a-600x279.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a33c3969-a2ec-4710-acfb-367b46b3b10a.avif 973w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="dfe2e5" style="--dominant-color: #dfe2e5">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6f4b68d4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6f4b68d4" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="293" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/14f46e4a-3ce2-446d-bcc8-baf14f414f8a-300x293.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17751 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/14f46e4a-3ce2-446d-bcc8-baf14f414f8a-300x293.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/14f46e4a-3ce2-446d-bcc8-baf14f414f8a-600x586.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/14f46e4a-3ce2-446d-bcc8-baf14f414f8a.avif 744w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e4e6e6" style="--dominant-color: #e4e6e6">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-18555bb9 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="18555bb9" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">The Noise Test for robust algorithmic strategies</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3a1a5afc elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="3a1a5afc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Overfitting a strategy simply means fitting to the noise of the data and not to an underlying signal. The book <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Signal-Noise-Many-Predictions-Fail-but/dp/0143125087"><em>The Signal and the Noise</em></a> does a better job than I can differentiating the two. However, we want to find true signals not noise. Noise is random and unreliable.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-96e2aeb elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="96e2aeb" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									How can check if we have overfit to the noise in historical market data? We can noise-adjust the historical data by adding or subtracting various amounts of noise (volatility) to the historical price bars. This will create a new open, high, low, close series for us to test our strategy on.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4b3f1824 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="4b3f1824" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="282" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/3b6ceac4-df6d-4aa9-a56e-133a0664fe7e-300x282.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17752 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/3b6ceac4-df6d-4aa9-a56e-133a0664fe7e-300x282.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/3b6ceac4-df6d-4aa9-a56e-133a0664fe7e.avif 522w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e3dedf" style="--dominant-color: #e3dedf">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-d755379 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="d755379" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="408" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/600f4834-7ebc-4c4f-ac9f-33ee6434d98c.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17753 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/600f4834-7ebc-4c4f-ac9f-33ee6434d98c.avif 675w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/600f4834-7ebc-4c4f-ac9f-33ee6434d98c-300x191.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/600f4834-7ebc-4c4f-ac9f-33ee6434d98c-600x382.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="ecebf1" style="--dominant-color: #ecebf1">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-475f774a elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="475f774a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="415" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/441d553a-d8ad-4128-80a3-a9e99d20918a.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17754 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/441d553a-d8ad-4128-80a3-a9e99d20918a.avif 655w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/441d553a-d8ad-4128-80a3-a9e99d20918a-300x195.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/441d553a-d8ad-4128-80a3-a9e99d20918a-600x389.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="ecebf1" style="--dominant-color: #ecebf1">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4bdaf974 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4bdaf974" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<em>A strategy waiting for four straight green closes to initiate a short entry would take a trade on the original data, but have the trade skipped in these two noise-adjusted variations at the circled bar.</em>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3b92a006 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="3b92a006" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Repeating this noise test to generate 1,000 or more new noise-adjusted data sets is a good start. Then we can re-trade the same strategy 1,000 times and view how the same strategy would perform if the noise wasn’t exactly how it was in the historical data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5f82f6ad elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5f82f6ad" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If the noise adjusted results are no longer profitable, then <strong>the strategy was fit to the historical noise</strong>. If the noise test remains profitable then we can have increased confidence our strategy was not overfit to the historical noise.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-45330a4c elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="45330a4c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="292" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/NoiseTest-1-300x292.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17755 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/NoiseTest-1-300x292.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/NoiseTest-1-600x584.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/NoiseTest-1.avif 623w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e0dee2" style="--dominant-color: #e0dee2">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2972f133 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2972f133" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									To read more about the specifics of the noise test please check out a couple of these articles I wrote								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4b2e6f1 elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="4b2e6f1" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<a href="https://buildalpha.com/noise-test/">

												<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Noise Testing Your Trading Strategies – Build Alpha</span>
											</a>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
											<a href="https://www.seeitmarket.com/system-traders-the-noise-test-and-lying-backtests-16997/">

												<span class="elementor-icon-list-icon">
							<svg aria-hidden="true" class="e-font-icon-svg e-fas-circle" viewBox="0 0 512 512" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M256 8C119 8 8 119 8 256s111 248 248 248 248-111 248-248S393 8 256 8z"></path></svg>						</span>
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">Lying Backtests and the Noise Test – Featured on See It Market</span>
											</a>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3f64a818 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="3f64a818" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Monte Carlo Simulation for Trading Strategy Development</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-22ba6d7c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="22ba6d7c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The most popular robustness testing method. There are many variations and uses of Monte Carlo Simulations and Monte Carlo analysis so let’s break down a few below:								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-41d1fc1c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="41d1fc1c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<strong>Reshuffle – </strong>reshuffle the order of historical trades and repeat 1,000 times to create 1,000 new equity curves. Since we are reshuffling the order, we end at the same spot, but the path changes, showing how our account could have fared if the trades did not play out in the sequence they did. You will notice a larger maximum drawdown than the backtest.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2dd72026 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2dd72026" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<strong>Resample – </strong>randomly select a historical trade until you reach your backtest’s total trade count. You can randomly select the same trade aka resampling with replacement. Repeat 1,000 times until you have 1,000 equity curves. Since we allow for the selection of the same trade, we do not end at the same spot. <strong>This test can provide more variation</strong> as the worst (or best) trade can be selected multiple times.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-3636d59f elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="3636d59f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="182" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/0e86ce91-9b12-4870-a291-f6fc4d872b82-300x182.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17756 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/0e86ce91-9b12-4870-a291-f6fc4d872b82-300x182.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/0e86ce91-9b12-4870-a291-f6fc4d872b82-600x364.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/0e86ce91-9b12-4870-a291-f6fc4d872b82.avif 746w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e9e7e5" style="--dominant-color: #e9e7e5">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-36b36a49 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="36b36a49" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="177" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/1d08a4bc-5096-4bda-976c-cb170a6fd4a2-300x177.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17757 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/1d08a4bc-5096-4bda-976c-cb170a6fd4a2-300x177.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/1d08a4bc-5096-4bda-976c-cb170a6fd4a2-600x355.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/1d08a4bc-5096-4bda-976c-cb170a6fd4a2.avif 756w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e8e6e6" style="--dominant-color: #e8e6e6">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-28cc2dfa elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="28cc2dfa" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Advanced Monte Carlo Tests for Trading Algorithms</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7766492c elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="7766492c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<strong>Randomized –</strong> We can randomize the entry and re-trade our strategy 1,000 times. On the other hand, we can re-trade our entries and randomize the exit. If your original strategy had no stop loss, then you should not randomly apply one. We should only randomly select and vary exit logic in the original strategy. If the Randomized Monte Carlo remains profitable, then it is likely we have true edge in our entry.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6b75f7bb elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6b75f7bb" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="278" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8ea71f11-780f-4b62-a6ea-9d60ffa1350f-300x278.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17758 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8ea71f11-780f-4b62-a6ea-9d60ffa1350f-300x278.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8ea71f11-780f-4b62-a6ea-9d60ffa1350f.avif 587w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="eeeaeb" style="--dominant-color: #eeeaeb">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-11f1cd31 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="11f1cd31" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<strong>Permutation – </strong>reshuffling the log of inter and intrabar price changes then exponentiating to recreate new bars. This creates synthetic data containing most of the statistical properties of our original data while destroying most of the patterns. We can re-trade our strategy on 1,000 new synthetic data series to see if our strategy holds up. This technique was popularized by Timothy Masters in his latest book <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Permutation-Randomization-Trading-System-Development/dp/B084QLXFKW" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><em>Permutation and Randomization Test for Trading System Development</em></a>.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-29bae623 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="29bae623" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Variance Testing for Trading Strategy Validation and analysis</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-689c53f5 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="689c53f5" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The variance test takes the historical trades and creates a probable distribution to resample. Next, the test selects random trades from this distribution until it reaches the backtest’s total trade count. It repeats this process 1,000 times.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4db5a2f4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4db5a2f4" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The trader can vary a performance metric, so the test only accepts variations that perform below the original backtest’s metric minus the trader’s input. <strong>The trader can now view how varying performance metrics affects the strategy’s variance</strong>.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6039e108 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6039e108" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									For example, assume the backtest had a win percentage of 61% and the trader set the test variation to 5%. The variance test would only display variations that had a win percentage of 56% or lower.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2789ff8a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2789ff8a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									This test allows a trader to view how a strategy would perform if things do not go as the backtest suggests they should. <strong>News flash – things never go as well as the backtest suggests</strong>. Now, the trader can answer if he would be comfortable trading this strategy in this less rosy situation.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-126c9f0d elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="126c9f0d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="919" height="410" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8b1bcc3d-cd40-4fea-9d6d-4b15bf064ba2.avif" class="attachment-full size-full wp-image-17759 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8b1bcc3d-cd40-4fea-9d6d-4b15bf064ba2.avif 919w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8b1bcc3d-cd40-4fea-9d6d-4b15bf064ba2-300x134.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8b1bcc3d-cd40-4fea-9d6d-4b15bf064ba2-768x343.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/8b1bcc3d-cd40-4fea-9d6d-4b15bf064ba2-600x268.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 919px) 100vw, 919px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e3e1e0" style="--dominant-color: #e3e1e0">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-34f89bbc elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="34f89bbc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									In the above image, we have two Variance Test results. The left side shows the strategy with 0% variation accepting all strategies from the simulation. The right-side shows variance test results with 5% variance selected – meaning only accepting strategies with win percentages 5% below the original backtest’s win percentage.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-25037c36 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="25037c36" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If live trading were to go 5% worse than our backtest, our strategy is a mere toss-up. A 50-50 bet is never how we want to risk hard earned capital. <strong>We want strategies that can maintain performance with slightly less optimistic metrics than the backtest</strong>.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6dec9534 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="6dec9534" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Delayed Testing</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-9d71737 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="9d71737" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Delaying a strategy’s entry or exit can be a useful test to determine we have not overlooked more optimal entry or exit points but can also tell us how fragile our strategy may be. If the strategy cannot enter 1 or 2 bars later or exit 1 or 2 bars later, especially for intraday, then the strategy may be showing signs of overfitting.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6588e4c9 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6588e4c9" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="576" height="595" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RobustnessTestingDelayedEntries-1.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17760 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RobustnessTestingDelayedEntries-1.avif 576w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/RobustnessTestingDelayedEntries-1-290x300.avif 290w" sizes="(max-width: 576px) 100vw, 576px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="e1e2e6" style="--dominant-color: #e1e2e6">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1dbf39d3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="1dbf39d3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Often a strategy may do better with a delayed entry as a strategy becomes known others begin to front run the entry and then bail when it does not work immediately. Build Alpha permits the option to add delayed entries to any strategy from the settings menu.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-545f2d28 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="545f2d28" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Liquidity Testing</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-27c00327 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="27c00327" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Large traders and institutions cannot execute their entire size on the open or the close. The liquidity test shows how a strategy would perform assuming the trader needed to enter from the close and until the next day’s close by taking an average of the two.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-339709d elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="339709d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
