									The liquidity test also tests the close and the next open and the close to the next midpoint. This test can also serve that if an entry or exit were to be missed does it make sense to put the trade on late.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6ae43c8f elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="6ae43c8f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="283" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/78b7e473-efa3-473f-b1d8-f0e42377a084-300x283.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17761 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/78b7e473-efa3-473f-b1d8-f0e42377a084-300x283.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/78b7e473-efa3-473f-b1d8-f0e42377a084-600x567.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/78b7e473-efa3-473f-b1d8-f0e42377a084.avif 666w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f4f3f6" style="--dominant-color: #f4f3f6">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-d3e1a57 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="d3e1a57" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									This example strategy showed promise, but if you cannot get the exact fills and need to scale-in or out due to size then this strategy shows a significantly different drawdown in both 2008 and 2020 market shocks. This could mean substantial risk to your initial investment.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2f4e379e elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="2f4e379e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Walk Forward Testing</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-79c2c337 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="79c2c337" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Walk forward tests smaller in and out of sample periods across the entire period tested on a rolling basis. Re-optimizing each in-sample period then trading the out-of-sample period with the selected parameter settings.</p><p>The test then combines all the out of sample periods as your final out of sample results (equity curve).</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2ffbdd11 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="2ffbdd11" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="559" height="236" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/b2e5573b-8db0-440e-bfce-7f30918458a6.avif" class="attachment-full size-full wp-image-17762 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/b2e5573b-8db0-440e-bfce-7f30918458a6.avif 559w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/b2e5573b-8db0-440e-bfce-7f30918458a6-300x127.avif 300w" sizes="(max-width: 559px) 100vw, 559px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="d0dfe3" style="--dominant-color: #d0dfe3">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7c9244fc elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="7c9244fc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Below you can see Build Alpha’s Walk Forward results window. The top row is the combined equity curve of all the out-of-sample periods. </p><p>Each row below contains the results from one of the out-of-sample periods or “splits”. This allows traders to inspect as granularly as needed.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-50c0c6c elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="50c0c6c" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="1326" height="737" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a.avif" class="attachment-full size-full wp-image-17763 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a.avif 1326w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a-300x167.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a-1024x569.avif 1024w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a-768x427.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/20aee652-5269-4352-8925-b107445b3c1a-600x333.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 1326px) 100vw, 1326px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f7f7f9" style="--dominant-color: #f7f7f9">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-676d82bc elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="676d82bc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h4 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Is Walk Forward a valid approach?</h4>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2705c0cf elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2705c0cf" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Walk forward testing, popularized by Bob Pardo, is a popular approach to robustness testing <strong>but it actually</strong> <strong>introduces a handful of new risks</strong>. The many downsides and pitfalls are discussed in the book <a href="https://www.amazon.com/Advances-Financial-Machine-Learning-Marcos/dp/1119482089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><em>Advances in Financial Machine Learning (Pardo, 2018)</em></a>.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2fbb353f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2fbb353f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Mainly, walk forward analysis only tests a single price path whereas other tests such as Noise testing, Vs Shifted, Variance testing or Monte Carlo Permutation test <strong><em>multiple</em></strong> price paths.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-123e0f2b elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="123e0f2b" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Additionally, due to test design, walk forward analysis splits the data many times creating an increase in data leakage. That is where data from an in-sample period is used during an out-of-sample period. </p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-434c3b5d elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="434c3b5d" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The simplest example is to imagine a trading strategy that uses a 200-period simple moving average. The first 199 bars after each data split will reference data from the previous in-sample period. More splits, more leakage.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5208dc5e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5208dc5e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p><strong>There are many other pitfalls to walk forward despite its popularity</strong>. Remember, robustness testing is about finding what will provide a strong Sharpe ratio and trading performance in the probable future not what has done so on the known historical price path.</p><p>The full <a href="https://buildalpha.com/walk-forward-optimization">Walk Forward Testing</a> article is here.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2a241e74 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="2a241e74" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Robustness testing parameters through Sensitivity Analysis</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-b32a3f1 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="b32a3f1" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Optimizing parameters often leads to more fragile strategies as the risks of overfitting often skyrocket. However, how can we find robust parameters? The simplest rule of thumb is to look for parameters areas that all show similar performance.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-335d0e01 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="335d0e01" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									That is, if the best parameter setting is 20 then we should aim to see at least +/- 10% showing about as good of performance. In this case, we want to see values 18 to 22 produce similar backtest metrics.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-47985368 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="47985368" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If the parameter values next to the optimal parameter setting causes a large drop in performance, then the optimal parameter setting is too fragile and likely just overfit to the historical data. This trouble can be easily avoided by using 3D surface graphs.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-7badf493 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="7badf493" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="300" height="262" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/62b609be-a35f-43f1-a2d5-9e82de6186cf-300x262.avif" class="attachment-medium size-medium wp-image-17764 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/62b609be-a35f-43f1-a2d5-9e82de6186cf-300x262.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/62b609be-a35f-43f1-a2d5-9e82de6186cf-600x524.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/62b609be-a35f-43f1-a2d5-9e82de6186cf.avif 742w" sizes="(max-width: 300px) 100vw, 300px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="ecd2d1" style="--dominant-color: #ecd2d1">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-14d9ded0 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="14d9ded0" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Choosing a parameter value here is too risky as the performance nearby drops significantly. On the other hand, choosing a parameter value in the red section provides a greater perceived margin of safety as the neighboring values show similar performance.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-153c3512 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="153c3512" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Noise Test Parameter Optimization for trading strategies</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-57afffd9 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="57afffd9" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Build Alpha takes parameter optimization one step farther equipping you with an additional robust check to the parameter selection process. <strong>Build Alpha enables the trader to add noise-adjusted samples to the optimization process</strong> so Build Alpha will find what parameter settings work best across all the noise-adjusted sample.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1976bdc elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="1976bdc" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="475" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/c822c795-f827-4295-9408-972b78064b61-768x570.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17765 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/c822c795-f827-4295-9408-972b78064b61-768x570.avif 768w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/c822c795-f827-4295-9408-972b78064b61-300x223.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/c822c795-f827-4295-9408-972b78064b61-600x446.avif 600w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/c822c795-f827-4295-9408-972b78064b61.avif 975w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="eaedf2" style="--dominant-color: #eaedf2">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-35e9a645 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="35e9a645" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Combining parameter values in a stable region with good performing neighbors and the great performance across noise adjusted data sets then we are doing as much as we can to shift the probabilities in our favor.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-54a8495a elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="54a8495a" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									The full article with a case study can be found here: <a href="https://buildalpha.com/noise-test-parameter-optimization/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Noise Test Parameter Optimization</a>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5c81a6b1 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="5c81a6b1" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Stress Testing or Scenario Testing with Synthetic Data</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-40b80494 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="40b80494" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									<p>Market shocks are often what gets system traders in trouble. Build Alpha now lets you generate synthetic data with your desired “shocks” to test your strategy against.</p><p>You can choose various data generation methods such as noise adjusted data and monte carlo permutated data and also create custom rules such as the synthetic data must have a larger drawdown than the historical data or must take longer to recover from its max drawdown or a whole host of other ideas.</p><p>After hitting test, Build Alpha will generate synthetic data sets and only test your strategy against the ones that match your desired rules.</p><p>Stress Testing rules can be combined or treated as unique, individual rules. In the latter case, Build Alpha will give each rule its own unique color in the plot so you can evaluate strategy performance across your rule set.</p><p>Summary statistics are provided for each rule below the display.</p>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2d4c8bf elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="2d4c8bf" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="640" height="380" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTest-1.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17766 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTest-1.avif 762w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTest-1-300x178.avif 300w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTest-1-600x356.avif 600w" sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, 640px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="f0f1f1" style="--dominant-color: #f0f1f1">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-79263b66 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="79263b66" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">
															<img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="568" height="522" src="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTestGraph-1.avif" class="attachment-medium_large size-medium_large wp-image-17767 not-transparent" alt="" srcset="https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTestGraph-1.avif 568w, https://www.buildalpha.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/StressTestGraph-1-300x276.avif 300w" sizes="(max-width: 568px) 100vw, 568px" data-has-transparency="false" data-dominant-color="d9dee4" style="--dominant-color: #d9dee4">															</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2e0d9bc4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="2e0d9bc4" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Should my trading strategy pass every robustness test?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-5a239c42 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="5a239c42" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									In a perfect world, yes. However, as algorithmic traders, we rarely live in a perfect world. Robustness testing is to help us avoid avoidable catastrophe but is not a crystal ball, unfortunately.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-efd3a0f elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="efd3a0f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									We can never prevent overfitting or truly know if we have not overfit. <strong>All we can do is attempt to lower the probabilities that we have overfit</strong>, and these stress-test methods help.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-92c4d24 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="92c4d24" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Trend following strategies tend to have lower win rates as they look for large, outsized moves. Running a trend following strategy through the variance test while reducing the win percentage does not have much of an affect. On the other hand, adjusting the noise for a trend following strategy seems applicable.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-27183491 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="27183491" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Thinking through what each test is testing and what your strategy is attempting to capture can help find the right tests for each specific strategy.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-455c68f4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="455c68f4" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Remember, putting strategies on a simulated account or trading with reduced size can be another form of checking robustness on live data.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6b63bec3 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="6b63bec3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Robustness testing on my trading portfolio or on individual strategies?</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2992c2eb elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2992c2eb" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Yes, you should do robustness testing on both individual strategies and your portfolio. Build Alpha provides the tools to run all the robustness checks and tests on both individual strategies and portfolios of strategies.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4b2ee2ee elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="4b2ee2ee" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									In the previously linked Algorithmic Trading Guide I shared my thoughts on how to build a proper trading portfolio. In this guide, <strong>I make the case that bad standalone strategies may have a place in your portfolio</strong> as they can “do the dirty work” to assist your star players. These role player strategies will rarely pass robustness tests; however, adding them to your portfolio should not harm the robustness tests of your portfolio!								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-56f7f612 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="56f7f612" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Robustness Testing Takeaways</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-36395dbe elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="36395dbe" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">1. Most algorithmic traders fail because they overfit strategies causing loss of risk capital</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">2. Algorithmic traders would do better incorporating robustness tests and checks</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">3. There is no best robustness test. Each test tests something different.</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">4. A backtest is often not enough. Robust strategies require additional testing.</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">5. Passing every test is ideal but not required for a robust trading strategy</span>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-163d826f elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="163d826f" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Need to Knows</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-158adfd3 elementor-icon-list--layout-traditional elementor-list-item-link-full_width elementor-widget elementor-widget-icon-list" data-id="158adfd3" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="icon-list.default">
							<ul class="elementor-icon-list-items">
							<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">1. Backtesting is not enough to find robust trading algorithms</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">2. Robust Checks help identify when backtests are lying prior to the trader risking capital</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">3. Robustness tests aim to find trading systems that will weather changing market conditions</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">4. Overfitting and overoptimization can be reduced through robustness testing</span>
									</li>
								<li class="elementor-icon-list-item">
										<span class="elementor-icon-list-text">5. Strategies do not need to pass every robustness test to be considered robust</span>
									</li>
						</ul>
						</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4a496e81 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="4a496e81" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Summary: Robust Trading Strategies require additional testing</h2>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-560d0929 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="560d0929" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Many traders struggle to make algorithmic trading work, but when looking at trading software available it is clear. Robustness testing is not offered in nearly any software. That is why I set out to build Build Alpha and include the various robustness tests and robustness check I have come across over the past decade in my professional trading career. <strong>Strategies must survive multiple and ever-changing market conditions.</strong>								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-2ff541fe elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="2ff541fe" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									Robust trading systems are rare and will require the astute trader to discard many strategies in the process. However, discarding strategies before risking hard earned capital is a win in my book. I hope this guide helps you break your strategies before the market does. In the long run, running additional tests and avoiding losses where possible can have a tremendous impact on achieving your trading goals and how a strategy performs.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-418e292e elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="418e292e" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
									If you have any questions or additional insights, please feel free to email me anytime.								</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-16c6dbf7 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="16c6dbf7" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Author</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-572ca265 elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-id="572ca265" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="image.default">

									<a href="https://buildalpha.com/author-dave/">David Bergstrom</a> – the guy behind Build Alpha. I have spent a decade-plus in the professional trading world working as a market maker and quantitative strategy developer at a high frequency trading firm with a Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) seat, consulting for Hedge Funds, Commodity Trading Advisors (CTAs), Family Offices and Registered Investment Advisors (RIAs). I am a self-taught programmer utilizing C++, C# and python with a statistics background specializing in data science, machine learning and trading strategy development. I have been featured on Chatwithtraders.com, Bettersystemtrader.com, Desiretotrade.com, Quantocracy, Traderlife.com, Seeitmarket.com, Benzinga, TradeStation, NinjaTrader and more. Most of my experience has led me to a series of repeatable processes to find, create, test and implement algorithmic trading ideas in a robust manner. 						<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-d722d17 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="d722d17" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Risk Disclosure</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-a8a8e36 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="a8a8e36" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<p class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Futures And Forex Trading Contains Substantial Risk And Is Not For Every Investor. An Investor Could Potentially Lose All Or More Than The Initial Investment. Risk Capital Is Money That Can Be Lost Without Jeopardizing Ones Financial Security Or Life Style. Only Risk Capital Should Be Used For Trading And Only Those With Sufficient Risk Capital Should Consider Trading. Past Performance Is Not Necessarily Indicative Of Future Results.</p>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-1d17cac elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="1d17cac" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Hypothetical Performance Disclaimer</h3>				</div>
				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-16e0978 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-id="16e0978" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="heading.default">
					<p class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">Hypothetical Performance Results Have Many Inherent Limitations, Some Of Which Are Described Below. No Representation Is Being Made That Any Account Will Or Is Likely To Achieve Profits Or Losses Similar To Those Shown; In Fact, There Are Frequently Sharp Differences Between Hypothetical Performance Results And The Actual Results Subsequently Achieved By Any Particular Trading Program. One Of The Limitations Of Hypothetical Performance Results Is That They Are Generally Prepared With The Benefit Of Hindsight. In Addition, Hypothetical Trading Does Not Involve Financial Risk, And No Hypothetical Trading Record Can Completely Account For The Impact Of Financial Risk Of Actual Trading. For Example, The Ability To Withstand Losses Or To Adhere To A Particular Trading Program In Spite Of Trading Losses Are Material Points Which Can Also Adversely Affect Actual Trading Results. There Are Numerous Other Factors Related To The Markets In General Or To The Implementation Of Any Specific Trading Program Which Cannot Be Fully Accounted For In The Preparation Of Hypothetical Performance Results And All Which Can Adversely Affect Trading Results.</p>				</div>
					</div>
		</div>

					<script>
				const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
			</script>

<script type="text/javascript">
(function() {
  'use strict';

  // CONFIG ----------------------------------------------------
  const DELAY_MS = 30000;
  const SCROLL_PCT = 0.5;
  const SUPPRESS_DAYS = 30;
  const STORAGE_KEY = 'ba_mt_popup_seen';

  const DISCOUNT_AMOUNT = '$300';
  const DISCOUNT_PREFIX = 'MT4';

  // WordPress AJAX endpoint
  const FORM_ENDPOINT = '/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php';
  // -----------------------------------------------------------

  const popup = document.getElementById('ba-popup');
  if (!popup) return;

  let selectedAnswer = null;
  let selectedAnswerLabel = null;

  // --- Helpers ---
  function isSuppressed() {
    try {
      const seen = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
      if (!seen) return false;

      const seenAt = parseInt(seen, 10);
      if (isNaN(seenAt)) return false;

      const days = (Date.now() - seenAt) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);

      return days < SUPPRESS_DAYS;
    } catch (e) {
      return false;
    }
  }

  function markSeen() {
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, Date.now().toString());
    } catch (e) {}
  }

  function generateCode() {
    const chars = 'ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789';

    let suffix = '';

    for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) {
      suffix += chars.charAt(
        Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length)
      );
    }

    return DISCOUNT_PREFIX + '-' + suffix;
  }

  function showStep(n) {
    popup.querySelectorAll('.ba-step').forEach(el => {
      el.classList.toggle(
        'is-active',
        el.dataset.step === String(n)
      );
    });
  }

  function openPopup(force) {
    if (popup.classList.contains('is-open')) return;

    if (!force && isSuppressed()) return;

    popup.classList.add('is-open');

    document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden';

    setTimeout(() => {
      const firstOpt = popup.querySelector('.ba-opt');

      if (firstOpt) firstOpt.focus();
    }, 380);
  }

  function closePopup() {
    popup.classList.remove('is-open');

    document.body.style.overflow = '';

    markSeen();
  }

  // --- Step 1 ---
  const opts = popup.querySelectorAll('.ba-opt');
  const step1Next = document.getElementById('ba-step1-next');

  opts.forEach(opt => {

    opt.addEventListener('click', () => {

      opts.forEach(o => o.classList.remove('is-selected'));

      opt.classList.add('is-selected');

      selectedAnswer = opt.dataset.answer;

      selectedAnswerLabel = opt.querySelector('.ba-opt__title').textContent;

      step1Next.disabled = false;

    });

  });

  step1Next.addEventListener('click', () => {

    if (!selectedAnswer) return;

    const validationMap = {
      'overfitting': 'You\'re right — and it\'s the most common one.',
      'single_strategy': 'Yep — diversification across strategies matters.',
      'prebuilt': 'Exactly — you can\'t trust what you didn\'t build.'
    };

    document.getElementById('ba-validation-text').textContent =
      validationMap[selectedAnswer] || '';

    showStep(2);

    setTimeout(() => {

      const emailInput = document.getElementById('ba-email');

      if (emailInput) emailInput.focus();

    }, 380);

  });

  // --- Step 2 ---
  const emailInput = document.getElementById('ba-email');
  const step2Submit = document.getElementById('ba-step2-submit');
  const errEl = document.getElementById('ba-err');

  function isValidEmail(v) {
    return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(v.trim());
  }

  step2Submit.addEventListener('click', async () => {

    const email = emailInput.value.trim();

    if (!isValidEmail(email)) {

      errEl.classList.add('is-shown');

      emailInput.focus();

      return;

    }

    errEl.classList.remove('is-shown');

    step2Submit.disabled = true;

    step2Submit.innerHTML = 'Sending...';

    const code = generateCode();

    try {

      const formData = new FormData();

      formData.append('action', 'ba_send_popup_email');
      formData.append('email', email);
      formData.append('answer', selectedAnswer);
      formData.append('answer_label', selectedAnswerLabel);
      formData.append('discount_code', code);
      formData.append('page', window.location.pathname);

      const response = await fetch(FORM_ENDPOINT, {
        method: 'POST',
        body: formData
      });

      const result = await response.json();

      if (!result.success) {
        throw new Error(result.data || 'Email failed');
      }

      // Google Analytics
      if (window.gtag) {

        window.gtag('event', 'mt_popup_submit', {
          answer: selectedAnswer
        });

      }

      // Facebook Pixel
      if (window.fbq) {

        window.fbq('track', 'Lead', {
          content_name: 'MT Popup',
          value: 300
        });

      }

      // Show success
      showStep(3);

      markSeen();

    } catch (e) {

      console.error('Popup submit failed:', e);

      step2Submit.disabled = false;

      step2Submit.innerHTML = 'Send My $300 Off Code';

      alert('Something went wrong. Please try again.');

    }

  });

  emailInput.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {

    if (e.key === 'Enter') {
      step2Submit.click();
    }

  });

  emailInput.addEventListener('input', () => {

    if (
      errEl.classList.contains('is-shown') &&
      isValidEmail(emailInput.value)
    ) {

      errEl.classList.remove('is-shown');

    }

  });

  // --- Close handlers ---
  popup.addEventListener('click', (e) => {

    if (e.target === popup) {
      closePopup();
    }

  });

  document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {

    if (
      e.key === 'Escape' &&
      popup.classList.contains('is-open')
    ) {

      closePopup();

    }

  });

  // --- Triggers ---
  if (!isSuppressed()) {

    // Delay
    setTimeout(openPopup, DELAY_MS);

    // Scroll
    let scrollFired = false;

    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {

      if (scrollFired) return;

      const scrolled =
        (window.scrollY + window.innerHeight) /
        document.documentElement.scrollHeight;

      if (scrolled >= SCROLL_PCT) {

        scrollFired = true;

        openPopup();

      }

    }, { passive: true });

    // Exit intent
    let exitFired = false;

    document.addEventListener('mouseleave', (e) => {

      if (exitFired) return;

      if (e.clientY < 5) {

        exitFired = true;

        openPopup();

      }

    });

  }

  function resetState() {

    selectedAnswer = null;

    selectedAnswerLabel = null;

    popup.querySelectorAll('.ba-opt').forEach(o => {
      o.classList.remove('is-selected');
    });

    step1Next.disabled = true;

    emailInput.value = '';

    errEl.classList.remove('is-shown');

    step2Submit.disabled = false;

    step2Submit.innerHTML =
      'Send My $300 Off Code';

    showStep(1);

  }

  // Global debug helpers
  window.BAPopup = {

    open: () => {
      resetState();
      openPopup(true);
    },

    close: closePopup,

    reset: () => {

      try {
        localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);
      } catch (e) {}

      resetState();

    }

  };

})();</script>
<!-- end Simple Custom CSS and JS -->
<!-- start Simple Custom CSS and JS -->
<script type="text/javascript">

function openLb(src){document.getElementById('lb-img').src=src;document.getElementById('lb').classList.add('lb--open')}
document.addEventListener('keydown',function(e){if(e.kxey==='Escape')document.getElementById('lb').classList.remove('lb--open')});
document.querySelectorAll('.faq__q').forEach(function(q){q.addEventListener('click',function(){this.parentElement.classList.toggle('open')})});

// Countdown — 14 days from now
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function(){

  if (!document.getElementById('cd-d')) return;

  var end = new Date();
  end.setDate(end.getDate() + 14);

  function tick(){
    var now = new Date(),
        d = end - now;

    if (d <= 0) return;

    var days = Math.floor(d / 864e5),
        hrs  = Math.floor(d % 864e5 / 36e5),
        mins = Math.floor(d % 36e5 / 6e4),
        secs = Math.floor(d % 6e4 / 1e3);

    ['d','h','m','s'].forEach(function(k,i){
      var el = document.getElementById('cd-' + k);
      if (el) {
        var val = [days, hrs, mins, secs][i];
        el.textContent = String(val).padStart(2,'0');
      }
    });
  }

  tick();
  setInterval(tick, 1000);

});

function toggleOlder(btn) {
  const hidden = document.getElementById('olderVideos');
  const isShown = hidden.classList.toggle('show');
  btn.innerHTML = isShown
    ? 'Hide Older Videos <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="6 15 12 9 18 15"/></svg>'
    : 'Show Older Videos <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"/></svg>';
}

// Market tabs
document.getElementById('mktTabs').addEventListener('click', e => {
  if (!e.target.classList.contains('mkt-tab')) return;
  document.querySelectorAll('.mkt-tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.mkt-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.remove('active'));
  e.target.classList.add('active');
  document.querySelector(`[data-panel="${e.target.dataset.tab}"]`).classList.add('active');
});

// Expand/collapse
function toggle(id) {
  document.getElementById(id).classList.toggle('expanded');
}
